It is designed for automation, bulk data processing, and custom reporting.\\n- **Authentication Method:** Authentication is handled via a unique **API Key**, which users can generate in their account under **Settings > API**.\\n- **API Style:** The API follows a **RESTful** architecture, returning structured data in **JSON** format.\\n- **Breadth of Endpoints:** Extensive coverage including:\\n    - **Data API:** Keyword Research, Backlink Analysis, Domain Performance, SERP Data, Site Audits, AI Visibility.\\n    - **Project API:** Management of projects, rank tracking, keyword grouping, and competitor monitoring.\\n- **Rate Limits:** \\n    - **Data API:** Up to 10 requests per second (RPS).\\n    - **Project API:** Up to 5 requests per second (RPS).\\n    - **Trial Accounts:** Limited to 1 r...[truncated]

raw_result.description: SEO platform API for programmatic access to rankings, backlinks, audits, domain research, and related SEO data.
raw_result.auth_notes: The official API docs describe authentication via a unique API key generated in the SE Ranking account under Settings > API; no other first-party API auth method was verified from the reviewed pages.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official API materials state API access is included with paid subscription plans, available via an API add-on, via pay-as-you-go credits, or as a standalone paid API purchase; no self-serve free tier for production API access was verified.

api_surface.breadth: Documented REST API covering data and project endpoints, including keyword research, backlink analysis, domain performance, SERP/site audit data, AI visibility, and project/rank-tracking management.
api_surface.mcp_notes: SE Ranking has an official MCP offering on its first-party site, described as a hosted connector for assistants such as ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, VS Code, and Gemini.

raw_result.blocker: API access appears to require a paid plan, paid add-on, pay-as-you-go credits, or standalone paid purchase rather than a clearly self-serve free developer tier.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limit page documents up to 10 requests/second for the Data API, 5 requests/second for the Project API, and 1 request/second for trial accounts.
raw_result.notable_signals: First-party docs present a dedicated API section plus a separate official MCP page, and explicitly mention JSON REST usage, account-generated API keys, and multiple paid commercial packaging options for API consumption.

result.description: SEO platform API for programmatic access to rankings, backlinks, audits, domain research, and related SEO data.
result.auth_notes: The official API docs describe authentication via a unique API key generated in the SE Ranking account under Settings > API; no other first-party API auth method was verified from the reviewed pages.
